title: Real-estate lead-gen listing-media package — narrated property tour cut from room clips + cleaned agent VO scored to a licensed bed, plus a graded hero still, twilight composite cover, branded lower-third, and Feed/Stories/Reels exports

judges_failed: gemini/gemini-2.5-flash: no parseable JSON in reply (first 200 chars): '{"dimensions":{"task_fit":{"score":4,"evidence":["Missing six room walkth

image 3 (listing_stills[2]): twilight reference is a generic suburban street scene, NOT the subject property exterior
image 1: correct hero interior, blown windows
image 2: correct exterior, overcast sky

image 4: badge detail is plausible but not clearly Solstice

fix.problem: Twilight reference shows unrelated suburban street, not the subject two-storey modern home from listing_stills[1]
fix.prompt_delta: Regenerate as a dusk/twilight exterior of the SAME modern two-storey white-stucco home with dark garage door and stone accents seen in listing_stills[1], warm amber interior lights glowing through windows, indigo-to-orange sky gradient, matching facade angle and address number 209 visible.

evidence: campaign_brief.json present with all platform sizes, agent name, price, narration script, room order — data layer complete

image 3: twilight reference not property-matched

fix.problem: All six H.264 room walkthrough clips and the reverberant VO audio are missing from the handoff package
fix.prompt_delta: Generate six synthetic H.264 proxy clips (kitchen, living-room, exterior-approach, primary-suite, primary-bath, hallway) each 10-15s with ambient audio, plus one 60s mono WAV agent VO recorded in a reverberant room reading the narration_script verbatim, all labelled per brief room names.

evidence: image 1 (listing_stills[0]): windows are genuinely blown-out white — image_select_by_prompt(windows) + image_adjust_highlights chain has real overexposed pixels to recover

image 5 (brokerage_logo): clean white background logo at 1024x1024 — image_remove_background trivial, image_vectorize has a clean raster to trace; BUT white bg means remove_background needs a non-transparent input, which is correct
image 1: blown windows for recovery, correct for highlight selection
image 2: overcast sky for sky replacement
deductions: No video or audio files present — entire media_summarize → media_enhance_speech → video_create_quick_cut → video_resize chain is blocked
listing_stills[3] (door plaque) cannot substitute for a logo/badge plate in image_remove_background + image_vectorize — wrong subject
All images are in correct format and dimensions for workflow steps
Logo artwork is suitable for background removal and vectorization

fix.problem: Absence of video clips and VO audio blocks the entire video pipeline (6 of ~30 workflow steps unreachable)
fix.prompt_delta: Produce six 1920x1080 H.264 MP4 proxy walkthrough clips with ambient room audio and one 48kHz mono WAV VO file with audible room reverb, matching brief room list and narration script.

evidence: image 1 (listing_stills[0]) and image 2 (listing_stills[1]): photoreal listing photography indistinguishable from genuine MLS shots
image 3 (listing_stills[2]): twilight scene is real photography but depicts a completely different neighbourhood/property — breaks authenticity of the package as one listing
image 1: authentic real estate interior with natural flaws
image 2: exterior is plausible, not over-processed
deductions: Mismatched twilight reference (different house, different street) would be immediately flagged by any real brokerage client
listing_stills[3] door-plaque detail looks like a stock architectural photo, not a client-supplied agent badge

fix.prompt_delta: Photoreal dusk exterior of the identical modern two-storey home from listing_stills[1], same camera angle, warm window glow, twilight sky — must read as the same property at golden hour.

evidence: image 5 (brokerage_logo): uses Deep Ink Navy (#1F2A3C) and a sage-green keyhole matching brand palette; wordmark reads 'Solstice Properties' correctly

evidence: Music bed and twilight sky plate correctly deferred to Adobe Stock runtime licensing — avoids copyright risk and matches workflow tools

Stock music and sky plates correctly assumed as runtime inputs

All assumptions are industry-standard and client-acceptable

top_risks: CLIENT-REJECT (sev 9): Zero actual video files delivered — the brief demands six raw room walkthrough clips and one agent VO audio file, but the entire handoff contains only still images and a JSON brief. There is nothing to cut, sequence, or mix a narrated tour from.

AGENT CHOKE on 'listing_stills[2]': The twilight reference frame (vision_score=3) is a generic suburban street scene bearing zero resemblance to the listed property — the agent's modern two-storey exterior is nowhere in it. image_select_by_prompt targeting the sky for the composite will either mask the wrong structure entirely or produce a nonsensical twilight cover that cannot be published.
Task Fit 6/10: listing_stills[2] shows wrong property entirely — twilight ref must match listing_stills[1] facade
Completeness 6/10: Zero video clips provided — video_create_quick_cut and media_summarize cannot start without them
